title: pyrus pyraster var. rossica
intro: Pyrus pyraster var. rossica, also known as Russian pear or wild pear, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain amygdalin, a compound that releases cyanide when ingested, and its fruit, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Pyrus pyraster var. rossica include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to the potential toxicity of its seeds.
